steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Toomer uses figurative language to connect Washington, DC, with the rural South. The correct quotation must (1) use figurative language and (2) bridge both places — not describe just one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the southern wind / soil of my homeland passage: A southern wind carries soil of my homeland (the rural South) and it falls like a fertile shower upon DC streets. The simile links both places through a single image. ✓
  • Choice B: Describes DC alone — no figurative link to the rural South. ✗
  • Choice C: Describes DC alone — no figurative link to the rural South. ✗
  • Choice D: Describes train sounds in a valley — evokes the South but does not connect it to DC. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
